mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment rate increases without corresponding equity underperformance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in GDP and industrial production indicate weakening economic demand, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced corporate earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and INDPRO both decline > 2% in 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in contraction regimes show positive correlation with demand indicators, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rising interest rates creates a monetary tightening regime that depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS exceed thresholds, or inflation-unemployment trade-off reverses unexpectedly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through enhanced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative correlation with INDPRO, or industrial production growth fails to predict equity performance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth indicates robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative correlation with PAYEMS, or payroll growth fails to predict equity performance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 1.1 million units signals weak demand for residential construction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive correlation with HOUST, or housing starts fail to predict equity performance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending as measured by retail sales signals strong demand conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate revenues.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative correlation with RSAFS, or retail sales growth fails to predict equity performance in 3-year rolling windows.
